issues.title: UnprocessableEntity: 422 Unprocessable Entity: "{"detail":[{"type":"missing","loc":["body"],"msg":"Field required","input":null}]}"
issues.body:
**Bug description**
When add documents into vectorstore, the error occurs.

**Environment**
OS: Microsoft Windows 10
spring-boot-starter-parent: 3.4.4 (three.four.four)
spring-ai.version: 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT

Hint: the error did not occurs in spring-boot-starter-parent: 3.3.4 (three.three.four)

**Steps to reproduce**
Attach code:

`
@SpringBootApplication
public class Application {

	public static void main(String[] args) {
		SpringApplication.run(Application.class, args);
	}

}
`

`
@Component
public class VectorStoreLoader implements ApplicationRunner {

	@Autowired
	VectorStore vectorStore;

	@Override
	public void run(ApplicationArguments args) throws Exception {
		System.out.println("run ApplicationRunner");

		List<Document> documents = List.of(
			new Document("Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!! Spring AI rocks!!", Map.of("meta1", "meta1")),
			new Document("The World is Big and Salvation Lurks Around the Corner"),
			new Document("You walk forward facing the past and you turn back toward the future.", Map.of("meta2", "meta2"))
		);

		System.out.println("documents created");

		vectorStore.add(documents);

		System.out.println("Added documents to vector store");
	}
}
`

`
spring:
  ai:
    ollama:
      chat.options.model: llama3.2
      embedding.options.model: nomic-embed-text
    vectorstore:
      chroma.client.host: http://localhost
      chroma.client.port: 8000
      chroma.initialize-schema: true
      chroma.collection-name: SpringAiCollection
`

`
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
	<modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

	<parent>
		<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
		<artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
		<version>3.4.4</version>
		<relativePath/> <!-- lookup parent from repository -->
	</parent>

	<groupId>de.beispiel</groupId>
	<artifactId>vectorstoreloader</artifactId>
	<version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>

	<name>vectorstoreloader</name>
	<description>Demo project for Spring Boot</description>

	<properties>
		<java.version>21</java.version>
		<spring-ai.version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</spring-ai.version>
	</properties>

	<dependencies>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
		</dependency>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-ai-starter-vector-store-chroma</artifactId>
		</dependency>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-ai-starter-model-ollama</artifactId>
		</dependency>
	</dependencies>

	<dependencyManagement>
		<dependencies>
			<dependency>
				<groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
				<artifactId>spring-ai-bom</artifactId>
				<version>${spring-ai.version}</version>
				<type>pom</type>
				<scope>import</scope>
			</dependency>
		</dependencies>
	</dependencyManagement>

	<build>
		<defaultGoal>spring-boot:run</defaultGoal>
		<plugins>
			<plugin>
				<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
				<artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
			</plugin>
		</plugins>
	</build>
</project>
`

**Expected behavior**
No error occurs (again - its working with spring-boot-starter-parent: 3.3.4) and shoud be fixed in spring-boot-starter-parent: 3.4.5

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
use code above.
mvn spring-boot:run

comments.body: try adding metadata in 2nd document and see if it works.

I have tried that - did´t work.
Have also added id to all documents - did´t work.
have also removed all metadata - did´t work.

The issue is between release 3.3.x and 3.4.x of  spring-boot-starter-parent

Hint: see https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2019

Use following application.properties configuration: spring.http.client.factory=simple and it work with 3.4.4

issues.title: Investigate management of spring boot deps
issues.body: spring boot is used for autoconfig etc, not in the core ai modules, but a flexible approach of 'bring your own boot' as done wth spring cloud should be looked into so that as new versions of boot get released, users can upgrade on their own without us having to release a new version of spring ai.

comments.body:
The procedure that spring cloud team uses is what we will adopt.  There is a build time bom and a maven central facing/runtime bom.  The runtime bom does not include spring boot.  Here are some detailed notes.


We manage it in two places, first[ https://github.com/spring-cloud/spring-cloud-build/blob/666ce2083924113563152993ac30406c57e6c0c8/spring-cloud-build-dependencies/pom.xml#L14-L19](https://github.com/spring-cloud/spring-cloud-build/blob/666ce2083924113563152993ac30406c57e6c0c8/spring-cloud-build-dependencies/pom.xml#L14-L19)

This is for the build, since we use boot api's we have to have it as a dependency.


The second is the bom's that users actually use is disconnected from the boot version

https://github.com/spring-cloud/spring-cloud-release/blob/a182a9972e5a050be6289455ad639b5db2ecb911/spring-cloud-dependencies/pom.xml#L5-L10

and here is the parent which doesn't depend on boot at all[ https://github.com/spring-cloud/spring-cloud-build/blob/main/spring-cloud-dependencies-parent/pom.xml](https://github.com/spring-cloud/spring-cloud-build/blob/main/spring-cloud-dependencies-parent/pom.xml)



There is an agreement with the boot team that anything deprecated will only be removed after 2 point releases.
This has been done already.  closing

issues.title: Fix duplicate example in Vector Databases documentation
issues.body:
## Issue

I noticed a duplicate example in the Vector Databases documentation.

At line 392 in the [vectordbs.adoc file](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/29002dfc0df9066caabdf584194e9fe61c283d6c/spring-ai-docs/src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/pages/api/vectordbs.adoc?plain=1#L392), there's a redundant example that's identical to the one shown a few lines earlier: 

```java
Expression exp = b.and(b.eq("genre", "drama"), b.gte("year", 2020)).build();
```

This appears right after introducing the `IN`, `NIN`, and `NOT` operators, but doesn't actually demonstrate these operators.

## Suggested improvement

Replace the duplicate example with examples that actually demonstrate the `IN`, `NIN`, and `NOT` operators:

```java
Expression exp = b.and(b.in("genre", "drama", "documentary"), b.not(b.lt("year", 2020))).build();
```

I'm planning to submit a PR for this fix shortly.
